title: The Legend of Banh Chung and Banh Day
summary: The story of Lang Lieu and his filial piety, creating two types of cakes symbolizing Heaven and Earth.

slides.text: The sixth Hung King wanted to choose a successor, so he told his sons: 'Whoever finds the most meaningful offering to present to our ancestors will be given the throne'.
slides.imagePrompt: Hung King speaking to his many sons in ancient palace, royal setting, Vietnamese traditional architecture, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors, flat design

slides.text: The princes raced to find treasures and rare delicacies from everywhere. Only the 18th prince, Lang Lieu, poor and kind-hearted, was worried because he had nothing precious.
slides.imagePrompt: Prince Lang Lieu looking thoughtful and humble in a simple farming village, sunset, peaceful but concerned, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors, flat design

slides.text: One night, Lang Lieu dreamed of a god who told him: 'In heaven and earth, nothing is as precious as the grain of rice... Take glutinous rice to make cakes for the ancestors'.
slides.imagePrompt: Lang Lieu dreaming, a gentle golden spirit appearing in his dream, rice fields in the background, magical glow, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors, flat design

slides.text: Lang Lieu chose fragrant white glutinous rice, molded a round cake symbolizing Heaven, and a square cake symbolizing Earth, with bean and meat filling inside.
slides.imagePrompt: Lang Lieu making green square cakes (Banh Chung) and white round cakes (Banh Day), wrapped in leaves, simple kitchen, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors, flat design

slides.text: The King tasted them and found the cakes not only delicious but also filled with deep affection for the homeland. He decided to pass the throne to Lang Lieu.
slides.imagePrompt: Hung King tasting the cakes, smiling proudly, Lang Lieu standing nearby, villagers celebrating, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors, flat design

slides.text: Since then, every Tet holiday, Vietnamese people gather together to wrap Banh Chung and Banh Day to remember their roots.
slides.imagePrompt: A modern Vietnamese family gathering to wrap Banh Chung together, happy atmosphere, traditional Tet decorations, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors, flat design

slides.question: Which prince created Banh Chung and Banh Day?
What shape is Banh Chung and what does it symbolize?
What main ingredient did Lang Lieu use to make the cakes?
Why did the King choose Lang Lieu to inherit the throne?
options: Because Lang Lieu's cakes were very expensive
Because Lang Lieu was the strongest
Because Lang Lieu's offering was meaningful and filial
slides.answer: Because Lang Lieu's offering was meaningful and filial
